tools.name: bridges.addBridgeOutputs
tools.description: Adds outputs to an existing bridge.

tools.name: bridges.addBridgeSources
tools.description: Adds sources to an existing bridge.

tools.name: bridges.createBridge
tools.description: Creates a new bridge. The request must include one source.

tools.name: bridges.deleteBridge
tools.description: Deletes a bridge. Before you can delete a bridge, you must stop the bridge.

tools.name: bridges.describeBridge
tools.description: Displays the details of a bridge.

tools.name: bridges.listBridges
tools.description: Displays a list of bridges that are associated with this account and an optionally specified Arn. This request returns a paginated result.

tools.name: bridges.removeBridgeOutput
tools.description: Removes an output from a bridge.

tools.name: bridges.removeBridgeSource
tools.description: Removes a source from a bridge.

tools.name: bridges.updateBridge
tools.description: Updates the bridge

tools.name: bridges.updateBridgeOutput
tools.description: Updates an existing bridge output.

tools.name: bridges.updateBridgeSource
tools.description: Updates an existing bridge source.

tools.name: bridges.updateBridgeState
tools.description: Updates the bridge state

tools.name: entitlements.listEntitlements
tools.description: Displays a list of all entitlements that have been granted to this account. This request returns 20 results per page.

tools.name: flows.addFlowMediaStreams
tools.description: Adds media streams to an existing flow. After you add a media stream to a flow, you can associate it with a source and/or an output that uses the ST 2110 JPEG XS or CDI protocol.

tools.name: flows.addFlowOutputs
tools.description: Adds outputs to an existing flow. You can create up to 50 outputs per flow.

tools.name: flows.addFlowSources
tools.description: Adds Sources to flow

tools.name: flows.addFlowVpcInterfaces
tools.description: Adds VPC interfaces to flow

tools.name: flows.createFlow
tools.description: Creates a new flow. The request must include one source. The request optionally can include outputs (up to 50) and entitlements (up to 50).

tools.name: flows.deleteFlow
tools.description: Deletes a flow. Before you can delete a flow, you must stop the flow.

tools.name: flows.describeFlow
tools.description: Displays the details of a flow. The response includes the flow ARN, name, and Availability Zone, as well as details about the source, outputs, and entitlements.

tools.name: flows.grantFlowEntitlements
tools.description: Grants entitlements to an existing flow.

tools.name: flows.listFlows
tools.description: Displays a list of flows that are associated with this account. This request returns a paginated result.

tools.name: flows.removeFlowMediaStream
tools.description: Removes a media stream from a flow. This action is only available if the media stream is not associated with a source or output.

tools.name: flows.removeFlowOutput
tools.description: Removes an output from an existing flow. This request can be made only on an output that does not have an entitlement associated with it. If the output has an entitlement, you must revoke the entitlement instead. When an entitlement is revoked from a flow, the service automatically removes the associated output.

tools.name: flows.removeFlowSource
tools.description: Removes a source from an existing flow. This request can be made only if there is more than one source on the flow.

tools.name: flows.removeFlowVpcInterface
tools.description: Removes a VPC Interface from an existing flow. This request can be made only on a VPC interface that does not have a Source or Output associated with it. If the VPC interface is referenced by a Source or Output, you must first delete or update the Source or Output to no longer reference the VPC interface.

tools.name: flows.revokeFlowEntitlement
tools.description: Revokes an entitlement from a flow. Once an entitlement is revoked, the content becomes unavailable to the subscriber and the associated output is removed.

tools.name: flows.startFlow
tools.description: Starts a flow.

tools.name: flows.stopFlow
tools.description: Stops a flow.

tools.name: flows.updateFlow
tools.description: Updates flow

tools.name: flows.updateFlowEntitlement
tools.description: You can change an entitlement's description, subscribers, and encryption. If you change the subscribers, the service will remove the outputs that are are used by the subscribers that are removed.

tools.name: flows.updateFlowMediaStream
tools.description: Updates an existing media stream.

tools.name: flows.updateFlowOutput
tools.description: Updates an existing flow output.

tools.name: flows.updateFlowSource
tools.description: Updates the source of a flow.

tools.name: gatewayInstances.deregisterGatewayInstance
tools.description: Deregisters an instance. Before you deregister an instance, all bridges running on the instance must be stopped. If you want to deregister an instance without stopping the bridges, you must use the --force option.

tools.name: gatewayInstances.describeGatewayInstance
tools.description: Displays the details of an instance.

tools.name: gatewayInstances.listGatewayInstances
tools.description: Displays a list of instances associated with the AWS account. This request returns a paginated result. You can use the filterArn property to display only the instances associated with the selected Gateway Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

tools.name: gatewayInstances.updateGatewayInstance
tools.description: Updates the configuration of an existing Gateway Instance.

tools.name: gateways.createGateway
tools.description: Creates a new gateway. The request must include at least one network (up to 4).

tools.name: gateways.deleteGateway
tools.description: Deletes a gateway. Before you can delete a gateway, you must deregister its instances and delete its bridges.

tools.name: gateways.describeGateway
tools.description: Displays the details of a gateway. The response includes the gateway ARN, name, and CIDR blocks, as well as details about the networks.

tools.name: gateways.listGateways
tools.description: Displays a list of gateways that are associated with this account. This request returns a paginated result.

tools.name: offerings.describeOffering
tools.description: Displays the details of an offering. The response includes the offering description, duration, outbound bandwidth, price, and Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

tools.name: offerings.listOfferings
tools.description: Displays a list of all offerings that are available to this account in the current AWS Region. If you have an active reservation (which means you've purchased an offering that has already started and hasn't expired yet), your account isn't eligible for other offerings.

tools.name: offerings.purchaseOffering
tools.description: Submits a request to purchase an offering. If you already have an active reservation, you can't purchase another offering.

tools.name: reservations.describeReservation
tools.description: Displays the details of a reservation. The response includes the reservation name, state, start date and time, and the details of the offering that make up the rest of the reservation (such as price, duration, and outbound bandwidth).

tools.name: reservations.listReservations
tools.description: Displays a list of all reservations that have been purchased by this account in the current AWS Region. This list includes all reservations in all states (such as active and expired).

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: List all tags on an AWS Elemental MediaConnect resource

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Associates the specified tags to a resource with the specified resourceArn. If existing tags on a resource are not specified in the request parameters, they are not changed. When a resource is deleted, the tags associated with that resource are deleted as well.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description: Deletes specified tags from a resource.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
